title: podocarpus totara
intro: Podocarpus totara is a species of coniferous tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the Podocarpaceae family, often referred to as the yellow or New Zealand totara. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various forms for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Podocarpus totara are said to include the potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as provide relief from skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's properties can be harnessed through the use of essential oils, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
